name: Creole Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
Cake: Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Grease and flour a 9x13-inch baking pan or two 8-inch square cake pans.
In a small saucepan combine water, cocoa powder, and butter, stirring over low heat until melted. In a separate bowl mix buttermilk, baking soda, and eggs. Add to the flour and sugar mixture, then add the chocolate mixture and milk. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until cooked through in the pan. Turn out onto a rack to cool completely.
Filling: In a saucepan combine sugar, evaporated milk, egg yolks, and shortening. Cook over medium heat until the mixture comes to a boil, then remove from heat. Stir in vanilla extract, coconut, and pecans. Cool before spreading over the cake layer.
Frosting: Melt chocolate chips in the top of a double boiler over hot water. Remove from heat, stir in sour cream and salt. Beat with a wooden spoon until smooth. Allow to cool for 5 minutes before frosting the top and sides of the cakes. Refrigerate for one hour before serving.
